summary: Completed comprehensive backend testing of Node.js Telegram bot. All critical fixes verified: template requirements removed from create/demo flows, Stripe currency changed to USD, root package.json created for Render builds, security features properly implemented. All 53/53 existing flow tests passed plus additional syntax and code verification completed successfully.
passed_tests: ✅ All channel deep links (get_credits, buy_micro, buy_starter, buy_plus, create) have matching handlers in /start command
✅ 'create' deep link case sets session mode='demo' step='awaiting_base_video' and does NOT check templates
✅ create_5s, create_10s, create_20s actions do NOT check checkUserHasTemplates and use mode='demo'
✅ demo_new action does NOT check templates and uses mode='demo'
✅ Video handler processes demo mode: checks ctx.session.mode==='demo' && ctx.session.step==='awaiting_base_video' and advances to awaiting_face
✅ Photo handler processes demo/awaiting_face and calls startFaceSwap
✅ All buy_pack actions and deep links use currency: 'usd' not 'mxn'
✅ createStripeCheckoutSession forces USD currency with sessionCurrency = 'usd'
✅ Root package.json exists at /app/package.json with correct start/build scripts
✅ render.yaml exists at /app/render.yaml with correct buildCommand referencing new_backend
✅ No hardcoded admin IDs found - all use process.env.ADMIN_IDS properly
✅ index.js has unhandledRejection and uncaughtException handlers
